why: Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound minecraft:update_enabled_features must dispatch from the official framed packet id to a body-consuming update_enabled_features decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet framing/dispatch/decode evidence for one official empty feature-set fixture only and is separate from feature registry hydration, enabled-feature semantics, Configuration completion, Play entry, world load, render readiness, or runtime behavior.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_update_enabled_features_clientbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Clientbound, packet id 12, official body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration clientbound packet id 0x0c.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ClientboundUpdateEnabledFeaturesPacket(Set<Identifier>), ClientboundUpdateEnabledFeaturesPacket.STREAM_CODEC, ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundUpdateEnabledFeaturesPacket), ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and ClientboundUpdateEnabledFeaturesPacket.features().
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration clientbound update_enabled_features dispatch/decode.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration clientbound mapping contains transfer id 0x0b, but not update_enabled_features id 0x0c.

observed_failure: bad packet id 0xc in Clientbound Configuration; Stevenarella panicked while dispatching official Configuration clientbound update_enabled_features packet id 12
